author: y4rr
content:
Plan: Fix 5 distributed conformance bugs spanning 2 files.

#853 — all_gather fixture-key mismatch: fixture JSON uses 'input_shape' key; Fixture struct has no such field, tests fall through to f.shape which is None for this fixture. Fix: add input_shape field to Fixture struct, update test to use f.input_shape.

#854 — reduce_scatter fixture-key mismatch: same pattern — fixture has 'input_shape' key, test reads f.shape which is missing. Same fix.

#855 — DeviceMesh fixture key mismatch: fixture has 'shape'/'world_size' keys; test reads f.mesh_shape/f.mesh_world_size. Test-side fix: read f.shape and f.world_size (both already present in Fixture struct).

#856/#857 — SubBackend rank test: test calls create_group(4).into_iter().next() which gives rank=0. Then SubBackend::new(rank0_backend, [1,2,3]) fails because rank 0 is not in members [1,2,3] — correctly rejected. Fix: use the rank-1 backend (first member of [1,2,3]) as parent, and for rank_mapping test use rank-1 as well.

Files changed:
- ferrotorch-distributed/tests/conformance_distributed.rs (fixture key reads + cascade_skip removals)
No source-side changes needed for #856/#857 — SubBackend::new validation is correct.
